A collection of simple and interactive mini games including Tic Tac Toe, Rock Paper Scissors, Mood Color Changer, Flappy Bird, and a To-Do List. This repository focuses on basic programming concepts, logic building, and user interaction, making it ideal for beginners to learn and practice development skills.

The practical capstone version of GAME491 gives senior-level GAME students the opportunity to apply their learned skills in programming, art, game design, and other disciplines to a long-term, team based game development project that aims to emulate real world game development practices in a classroom setting.

This project is a 2D game developed using C++, focusing on implementing core game development concepts such as visual output handling, object interaction, and real-time game mechanics. The main goal of this project was to build a smooth and efficient game while applying fundamental programming and problem-solving skills.

Repository of the book examples and exercises. A project-based guide to 3D and immersive game development with Godot 4, designed for programmers with basic skills who want to create engaging games (including VR) while learning both technical proficiency and player-focused design.

This project is a classic Whack-a-Mole game clone designed to showcase core frontend development skills such as DOM manipulation, event handling, timing functions, and dynamic UI updates.  Moles appear randomly from different holes, and the player must click them quickly to earn points. The game challenges reaction speed and accuracy.

Fun Bros is a 2D platformer game built using the Godot engine, featuring smooth player movement, collision detection, basic enemy interactions, and level design to demonstrate core game development and logic skills.
GDLC — Game Development Life Cycle. Playbook + skill for persona-driven playtest cycles, triangulated findings, and ratchet-only-tightens. Sibling to SDLC-wizard and PDLC under XDLC.

🛡️ Gladiator: Rise of the Arena A fast-paced web-based action game where a gladiator battles soldiers, dragons, and bosses in an evolving arena. Built with scalable architecture, modular game logic, and interactive UI, showcasing real-world software engineering and game development skills.

# Unity Practice  This repository contains my Unity and C# practice scripts and small experiments.  ## Purpose To improve my game development skills by building and testing core gameplay mechanics in Unity.

Minimalist black-and-white pixel balloon browser game for my personal portfolio. Control the balloon by phone tilt and microphone blowing. Avoid dense obstacles to prevent game over. Reach the final flag to win. Built with Canvas and JavaScript, showing sensor, audio and game logic development skills.
The aim of this project is to create a fun rock paper scissors mobile game in react native as a portfolio project to build and showcase skills in mobile app development.

A modern and responsive developer portfolio built with React and Vite, featuring a futuristic glassmorphism UI, animated sections, dynamic typewriter effects, and smooth navigation. Showcases my skills, projects, game development interests, and software engineering journey.

This repository contains a collection of C projects showcasing skills in system programming, algorithms, and game development.   It includes real-world applications, AI-based logic, and interactive terminal games, demonstrating strong problem-solving and programming fundamentals.

Showcasing AI and software projects including a Tic-Tac-Toe AI using the Minimax algorithm. Demonstrates Python programming, algorithm design, game theory, and version control. FieldIQ Soccer is a structured player development program for serious athletes ages 10–18. Through small-group training and a focus on speed of play, decision-making, and confidence, players develop the skills that translate directly to game performance. Train smarter. Play faster.
